zh.lifeSummary: 當James Stewart 5th High Steward of Scotland於1243年3月2日出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾吉爾馬爾諾可時，他的父親Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland29歲，母親Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness34歲。他和Lady Egidia de Burgh of Ulster至少有4個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1309年7月16日在蘇格蘭艾斯爾凱爾享年66歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭雷福福爾佩斯利佩斯利 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When James Stewart 5th High Steward of Scotland was born on 2 March 1243, in Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland, his father, Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland, was 29 and his mother, Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness, was 34. He had at least 4 sons and 1 daughter with Lady Egidia de Burgh of Ulster. He died on 16 July 1309, in Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland, at the age of 66, and was buried in Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James Stewart 5th High Steward of Scotland est né le 2 mars 1243, à Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Écosse, son père, Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness, avait 34 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille avec Lady Egidia de Burgh of Ulster. Il est décédé le 16 juillet 1309, à Kyle, Ayrshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 66 ans, et a été enterré à Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als James Stewart 5th High Steward of Scotland am 2. März 1243 in Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland 29 und seine Mutter Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness 34. Er hatte mit Lady Egidia de Burgh of Ulster mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 16. Juli 1309 im Alter von 66 Jahren in Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland und wurde in Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando James Stewart 5th High Steward of Scotland è nato il il 2 marzo 1243 nel Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness, aveva 34 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia con Lady Egidia de Burgh of Ulster. Morì il 16 luglio 1309, nel Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland, all'età di 66 anni, e fu sepolto a Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: James Stewart 5th High Steward of Scotlandが1243年3月2日にスコットランドエイルシュアイアキルマルノックで生まれた時，父Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotlandは29歳，母Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchionessは34歳であった。彼はLady Egidia de Burgh of Ulsterとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1309年7月16日にスコットランドエイルシュアイアクイルで66歳で死去し，スコットランドレンフリューシュアイアペイスリーペイスリー アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: James Stewart 5th High Steward of Scotland(이)가 1243년 3월 2일 스컷랜드 에이르실르 킬마노크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness(은)는 34세였다. 그는 Lady Egidia de Burgh of Ulster(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1309년 7월 16일 스컷랜드 에이르실르 키일에서 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 파이슬리 파이슬리 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.2 March 1243–16 July 1309: 2 de março de 1243–16 de julho de 1309
pt.lifeSummary: Quando James Stewart 5th High Steward of Scotland nasceu em 2 de março de 1243, em Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland, seu pai, Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness, tinha 34 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha com Lady Egidia de Burgh of Ulster. Ele faleceu em 16 de julho de 1309, em Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland, com 66 anos, e foi sepultado em Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James Stewart 5th High Steward of Scotland родился 2 марта 1243 г. в местности килмарнокк, аиршир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness, 34. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь от Lady Egidia de Burgh of Ulster. Он умер 16 июля 1309 г. в местности кил, аиршир, Шотландия в возрасте 66 лет и был похоронен в местности пеисли эбби, пеисли, ренфрюшир, Шотландия.

es.2 March 1243–16 July 1309: 2 de marzo de 1243–16 de julio de 1309
es.lifeSummary: Cuando James Stewart 5th High Steward of Scotland nació el 2 de marzo de 1243, en Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland, su papá, Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness, tenía 34 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija con Lady Egidia de Burgh of Ulster. Murió el 16 de julio de 1309, en Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland, a la edad de 66 años, y fue sepultado en Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland.
